name: Sinsera RaspyJack (defensive, on-own-LAN)
description: DietPi image bundled with the operator's local ~/Downloads/Jack/ tree — RaspyJack as the upstream ships it. Installs run on first boot via the project's own install_raspyjack.sh. Authorization: USE ONLY ON HARDWARE YOU OWN AND NETWORKS YOU HAVE WRITTEN PERMISSION TO TEST.

preconfigured.source_payload: raspyjack-src.tar.gz (108 MB) — embedded on the boot partition

first_boot_sequence: DietPi finishes its standard first-boot setup
log in as root via SSH (key-based) and run /opt/raspyjack/raspyjack.py
authorization_notice: RaspyJack is dual-use network tooling. By flashing this image you confirm: (1) the hardware is yours, (2) the network where you'll operate it is yours OR you have written authorization to test it, (3) you will not use it against third-party systems. The Ark Hub does NOT call any RaspyJack offensive endpoints — those are operator-controlled on the Pi only.
